mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds 5% annually while CPI growth remains below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side strength, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equities and yields positive returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative or flat in more than 70% of periods following industrial production acceleration during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product confirms expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods following positive GDP growth readings.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the consumer price index triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 60% of periods following CPI spikes above the threshold, indicating inflation is priced as demand-driven rather than supply-constrained.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns when payroll growth exceeds 200k monthly while unemployment falls below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader economic slowdown, prompting defensive portfolio rebalancing that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines are followed by positive equity returns in more than 65% of cases during late-cycle phases, indicating the signal is overridden by monetary policy support.
